title: What bold actions have I been delaying
subtitle: A journal set for identifying where overthinking has replaced forward movement.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Day 44 of the MONEY program. The prompts trace the specific actions that have been stalled by fear of the wrong move.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 44 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when there's a specific money decision or action you've been circling without landing.

content.text: A journal set from Day 44 of the MONEY program. The prompts don't require you to take the action — they ask you to name what's in the way of it.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
Where are you right now with taking bold action around money?
Write a paragraph. No assessment required — just a report from where you are today.
Think of a time you took bold action around money and it worked out. What did that feel like?
Write what you remember. Concrete details count more than the feelings here.
What actions around money, work, or investment have you been delaying?
What are you afraid might happen if you move forward?
Write the actual fear, not the polished version. "I'm afraid I'll look stupid" is more useful than "I'm not sure it's the right time."
What one action could you take today, however small, that moves you forward on one of those?
Write it. Then write when you will do it.
Adapted from Day 44 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
